name: Forks State Forest Campground
description: Rustic Boardman River campground south of Williamsburg with paddle-in access and Blue Ribbon trout water.

name: Platte River State Forest Campground
description: Rustic state forest campground on the Platte River near Honor with paddling and fishing access.

name: Grass Lake State Forest Campground
description: Rustic campground on Grass Lake Flooding managed by Colfax Township with Betsie River access.

name: Lake Dubonnet State Forest Campground
description: Large rustic campground northwest of Interlochen on Lake Dubonnet with direct access to Lost Lake Pathway.

name: Schecks Place State Forest Campground
description: Rustic Boardman River campground on the Shore-to-Shore Trail southwest of Williamsburg.

name: Schecks Place Trail Camp
description: Large group/equestrian trail camp on the Boardman River and Shore-to-Shore Trail.

name: D.H. Day Campground
description: Rustic Sleeping Bear Dunes campground with vault toilets and access to Lake Michigan and the Heritage Trail.

name: Platte River Campground
description: Modern Sleeping Bear Dunes campground with showers, electric sites and Platte River access.

name: Pere Marquette State Forest dispersed area - Muncie Lakes unit
description: Provisional dispersed/public-land camping pin in the Muncie Lakes area. DNR allows backpacking or car camping on state forest land when the site is more than 1 mile from a state forest campground and otherwise follows posted rules.

name: Huron-Manistee dispersed area - Boardman corridor
description: Provisional national-forest dispersed/public-land camping pin in the Boardman corridor. Huron-Manistee says dispersed camping outside designated campgrounds does not require a fee or permit, subject to local restrictions and closures.
